# Investor & Founder Matrix — Coastal Alpine Tech (CAT)

**Version:** 1.0.0 · **Date:** 2026-07-13  
**Purpose:** Map what **investors**, **founders**, and **co-founders** optimise for across stages — and how CAT should position using public patterns from NZ and global startups (**Halter**, **Sharesies**, **Cursor/Anysphere**).  
**Companion:** [FUNDING_ELIGIBILITY_MATRIX.md](FUNDING_ELIGIBILITY_MATRIX.md)  
**Machine table:** [matrices/investor_expectations.csv](matrices/investor_expectations.csv)

> **Disclaimer:** Educational synthesis from **public** articles and company disclosures. Not an offer of securities. Comp valuations and metrics change; always re-check primary sources. CAT is not claimed to be comparable in valuation or scale to any named company.

---

## 1. Capital stack: who wants what

| Actor | Primary goal | Typical check size (indicative) | Needs from CAT | Hard “no”s |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Founder** | Mission + control + runway to proof | — | Non-dilutive first; clean governance | Forced cloud pivot that breaks sovereignty |
| **Co-founder** | Equity fairness + role clarity + velocity | — | Vesting, IP assignment, decision rights | Ambiguous equity; unscoped “CTO of everything” |
| **Angel** | Outsized equity for early risk; team bet | Small–mid (varies widely) | Story, team, early proof, large market | No team; pure idea with no edge |
| **Pre-seed / seed VC (NZ)** | Fund-returning outcome; deeptech/agri optionality | Fund-dependent | Venture path, NZ advantage, coachability | Lifestyle biz; no export path |
| **Series A+ agritech VC** | Scale field systems; unit economics | Larger | ARR/pilots, multi-site, expansion geo | Science project without sales motion |
| **Global AI growth VC** | Category-defining software velocity | Very large | Usage, revenue acceleration, platform | Slow hardware cycles only |
| **Strategic corporate** | Distribution / data / product synergy | Pilot → equity | Integration roadmap, procurement fit | Competing core product without deal |
| **Gov / grant funder** | Public outcomes (jobs, exports, R&D, Māori outcomes) | Co-fund rules | Eligibility, reporting, additionality | BAU opex; double-dip; weak evidence |
| **Impact / iwi-aligned capital** | Returns + tikanga / sovereignty | Varies | OCAP®, benefit-sharing, local residency | Extractive data models |

---

## 2. Stage ladder (investor lens)

Patterns synthesised from public fundraising education (e.g. [Startups.com series funding](https://www.startups.com/articles/series-funding-a-b-c-d-e), [angel primers](https://www.startups.com/articles/what-is-an-angel-investor)) and stage checklists used across VC writing (seed TAM expectations, Series A proof points).

| Stage | Investor focus | Traction bar (typical) | CAT mapping |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Pre-seed** | Team + insight + market | Prototype / design partners | **Now-ish** if team pack solid |
| **Seed** | Product + early pull + TAM | Paying pilots or strong LOIs; bottom-up market | **6–12 mo target** post pilots |
| **Series A** | PMF + repeatable sales | Often ~$1M+ ARR **or** strong multi-site proof in agritech | Later; after SOM proof |
| **Series B+** | Scale / geo / efficiency | Hard metrics, expansion | Halter-class field scale path |
| **Growth / late** | Category leadership | Large ARR, market share | Not applicable now |

### Startups.com-aligned signals (public education content)

| Theme | Public teaching point | CAT application |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Angels bet people early** | Angels often underwrite **founder/team** when metrics are thin; still care about market, product, competition | Lead with team + sovereignty insight; don’t fake ARR |
| **Series A needs traction KPIs** | Series A follows seed once user/revenue/KPI traction exists | Define CAT KPIs: edge nodes live, offline uptime, pilot farms, NRR |
| **Series B = PMF + expand** | Expansion capital after product-market fit | Domain portal expansion (water→soil→biosecurity) then AU |
| **Fundraising time** | Plan ~**3–4 months** for a proper raise | Don’t start equity raise same week as grant deadline chaos |
| **Seed equity** | Valuation/equity negotiated vs market, geo, strength of parties, growth/ARR | NZ deeptech norms; avoid Cursor-era AI valuations as comps |

Sources: [What Is An Angel Investor?](https://www.startups.com/articles/what-is-an-angel-investor), [Series A–E Funding](https://www.startups.com/articles/series-funding-a-b-c-d-e), [How Much Equity in a Seed Round?](https://www.startups.com/articles/how-much-equity-give-away-seed-round), [Raise Capital](https://www.startups.com/articles/raise-capital).

Score **importance to investor type**: ●●● critical · ●● important · ● nice · — n/a

| Criterion | Angel | NZ seed VC | Agritech A+ | AI growth VC | Strategic | Grant funder |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| **Founder–market fit** | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ●● | ●● | ●● |
| **Team completeness** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ●● |
| **TAM ≥ venture scale / path to large revenue** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ● |
| **Bottom-up SAM/SOM** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ●● | ● |
| **Technical moat / deeptech** | ●● | ●●● | ●● | ●● | ●● | ●●● (R&D) |
| **Product demo / shipping cadence** | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● |
| **Paying customers / ARR** | ● | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ● |
| **Pilot / field proof** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ● | ●●● | ●● |
| **Unit economics path** | ● | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ● |
| **GTM / distribution** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ● |
| **Data sovereignty / trust** | ● | ●● | ●● | ● | ●● | ●●● |
| **Regulatory readiness** | ● | ●● | ●● | ● | ●●● | ●●● |
| **Export / multi-geo plan** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● | ●● | ●● (exports priority funds) |
| **Governance / board readiness** | ● | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●● |
| **ESG / Māori outcomes** | ● | ● | ● | — | ● | ●●● (MDF etc.) |
| **Capital efficiency** | ●● | ●●● | ●● | ● | ●● | ●●● |
| **Exit narrative** | ●● | ●●● | ●●● | ●●● | strategic fit | — |

### CAT readiness vs criterion (self-score 0–5)

| Criterion | CAT now (est.) | Gap to close |
| :--- | ---: | :--- |
| Founder–market fit | 3 | Public founder narrative pack |
| Team completeness | 2–3 | Name GTM + domain advisors |
| TAM/SOM pack | 2 | Bottom-up model + pricing |
| Technical moat | 4 | Keep shipping; patents optional later |
| Shipping / demos | 4 | Field video + pilot dashboards |
| Paying traction | 0–2 | Paid pilots critical |
| Field proof | 1–2 | 2–3 named deployments |
| Unit economics | 1 | Hardware + SaaS contribution margin |
| GTM | 2 | Sales playbook per portal |
| Sovereignty story | 5 | Already a core asset — keep honest |
| Export plan | 2 | AU beachhead memo |
| Governance | 2 | Cap table, board observer norms |

---

## 4. Founder & co-founder matrix (what *you* should demand)

Fundraising is two-sided. Founders and co-founders should filter capital as hard as investors filter startups.

| Dimension | Founder wants | Co-founder wants | Good capital looks like | Bad capital looks like |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Mission integrity** | Sovereignty & rural resilience intact | Role still meaningful post-raise | Investor who accepts offline thesis | Force cloud-only for “scale optics” |
| **Control** | Enough votes to protect culture | Vesting fairness | Reasonable protective provisions | Full ratchet + hostile controls early |
| **Dilution** | Runway vs ownership balance | Pro-rata clarity | Stage-appropriate % | Overselling equity pre-traction |
| **Speed** | Fast decisions | Clear decision rights | Responsive partner | Endless DD theatre |
| **Help** | Customers, talent, NZ→global intros | Technical/hiring help | Value-add in agritech/AI | Logo-only money |
| **Follow-on** | Reserve for next round | Stability | Fund with dry powder | One-and-done microfund |
| **Cultural safety** | No extractive Māori data deals | Shared ethics | Impact-aligned terms | “Ignore TMR for growth” pressure |
| **Reporting load** | Sustainable | Not dual full-time IR jobs | Clean monthly metrics | Vanity metrics theatre |

### Co-founder readiness checklist (pre-raise)

- [ ] Written roles (CEO/tech/domain) and decision matrix  
- [ ] Vesting (e.g. 4yr/1yr cliff) + IP assignment to company  
- [ ] Conflict / exit / founder departure clauses  
- [ ] Shared position on Māori data and HITL (non-negotiables)  
- [ ] Agreement on non-dilutive-first vs equity-first sequencing  

---

## 5. Public comps relevant to CAT

### 5.1 Pattern table

| Company | Archetype | Public funding signals (indicative, press-reported) | What investors rewarded | CAT lesson |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Halter** | NZ **field systems + software** for livestock; virtual fencing / animal management | Multi-round scale: e.g. Series D ~NZ$165M at ~NZ$1.65B (2025, led BOND; Blackbird, Icehouse, Bessemer, DCVC et al.); later Series E reporting at ~US$2B / multi-hundred-M raises with Founders Fund participation; **~1M collars** sold in public commentary | Global expansion, category reframe beyond “small agtech”, hard field deployment scale, top-tier global + NZ VCs | **NZ agritech can attract global capital** when field hardware+software proves multi-country ops — CAT should sell **field edge mesh**, not “NZ research project” |
| **Sharesies** | NZ **consumer fintech** wealth platform | e.g. 2021 ~NZ$50M at ~NZ$500M (Amplo-led; Icehouse, Even, Benton et al.); later NZ press on ~NZ$750M valuation rounds; large local user penetration | Trust, distribution density in NZ, product simplicity, AU expansion | **Trust + local density** matters; less product-analogous to CAT B2B edge, more **brand/trust** lesson for consumer-facing portals |
| **Cursor (Anysphere)** | US **AI developer tool** | Rapid multi-round climb (public: Series A–D arc into multi-billion valuations; e.g. Series D **$2.3B at $29.3B** Nov 2025 with Thrive, a16z, Accel, Coatue, Nvidia, Google participation per company/press) | Extreme product usage, revenue velocity, category ownership in AI coding | **Software agent velocity** is a different game; CAT’s Aether can borrow **agent UX narrative** but must not use Cursor multiples as valuation comps |

### 5.2 Acquisition / late-stage trend notes (public)

- **Late-stage AI software** attracts strategic capital (cloud/chip/platform cos.) and can move to **strategic acquisition** discussions when category leadership is clear (monitor press; treat rumours cautiously).  
- **Agritech hardware+software** more often scales via **expansion equity** and long field cycles; bankruptcies and down-rounds in global agrifoodtech (AgFunder commentary on sector funding down from 2021 peak) mean investors now demand **proof of durable field economics**.  
- **NZ deeptech policy capital** (e.g. NZGCP Aspire weighting toward agri-tech, AI/ML, clean-tech) signals government co-investor appetite for CAT-like themes at **seed**, not Halter-scale alone.

### 5.3 Investor brands that appear around CAT-adjacent stories (for research, not outreach spam)

| Cluster | Examples (public) | Relevance |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| NZ early-stage | Icehouse Ventures, Blackbird (AU/NZ activity), Outset-type deeptech, NZGCP Aspire | Natural first equity conversations post-pilots |
| Global agritech / deep | Bessemer, DCVC, Founders Fund (via Halter story), BOND | Later; need scale proof |
| AI platform | a16z, Thrive, Accel, Coatue, strategics (Nvidia/Google in Cursor story) | Only if software usage metrics go nonlinear |
| Gov/non-dilutive | MBIE Innovation Services, MPI PSGF, TPK MDF | **Now** |

## 6. Two-sided scorecard: CAT × investor type

| Investor type | CAT attractiveness (now) | Investor attractiveness to CAT | Engage? |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| Angels (agri/deeptech NZ) | 6/10 | 8/10 | **Yes** after pilot LOIs |
| NZ seed / deeptech | 5/10 | 8/10 | **Yes** with SOM + pilots |
| Global agritech | 3/10 | 7/10 | **Later** |
| Global AI growth | 2/10 | 5/10 | **No** (wrong stage/metrics) |
| Strategic ag/telco | 4/10 | 7/10 | **Pilot $** first |
| Grant funders | 8/10 | 9/10 | **Yes — primary path now** |
| Impact / Māori-aligned | 5/10* | 9/10 | **Yes if partnership real** |

\* rises sharply with authentic kaitono structure.

---

## 7. Pitch narrative by audience (same company, different slide)

| Audience | Lead with | Prove with | Avoid |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Grant (MBIE R&D)** | Technical uncertainty + capability build | Method, milestones, R&D definition tests | Pure commercial growth brag |
| **Grant (MPI PSGF)** | Primary sector growth + farm outcomes | Co-invest, economic benefit, pilots | Abstract AI hype |
| **Grant (TPK)** | Māori economic outcomes | Kaitono, ROI, sustainability | Tech demo without people outcomes |
| **Angel** | Team + insight + unfair advantage | Demo, early users, market | 40-page compliance dump first |
| **Seed VC** | Wedge → platform + export | SOM, pipeline, unit econ sketch | “We’re the NZ Cursor” unearned |
| **Agritech A** | Multi-farm metrics | Retention, expansion revenue | Science-only roadmap |
| **Co-founder recruit** | Mission + equity + craft** | Stack quality, HITL culture | Undefined role |

---

## 8. Metrics dashboard investors will ask for

| Metric | Why | CAT definition (proposed) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Edge nodes deployed** | Field scale proxy (Halter: collars shipped) | RPi nodes in production-like use |
| **Offline decision rate** | Differentiator | % inferences without cloud |
| **Pilot farms / orgs** | Agritech proof | Paying or formal pilot agreements |
| **ARR / pipeline** | Equity readiness | SaaS + support; hardware separate |
| **Gross margin path** | Hardware risk | Target software-attach mix |
| **Time-to-value** | Sales motion | Days from unbox to first insight |
| **Security/compliance milestones** | Trust | Checklist %; not fake certification |
| **NRR / logo retention** | PMF | Pilot renewal / expansion |

---

## 9. Negotiation cheat-sheet (founder side)

| Term | Prefer early | Watch-outs |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Instrument | SAFE/notes with clear caps vs premature priced round | Uncapped chaos without advice |
| Board | Founder majority pre-A | Investor control too early |
| Information rights | Reasonable monthly | Daily operational interference |
| Sovereignty covenants | Data residency commitments in side letter | “All data to US region for convenience” |
| Pro-rata | Protect founder/angel friends | Only large funds get pro-rata forever |
| Non-compete / IP | Company-owned IP | Founder IP left outside entity |

Seek NZ counsel before signing.

---

## 10. Synthesis: what “good” looks like for CAT in 12 months

| Stakeholder | Success picture |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Founders** | 18+ months runway mix (grant + revenue + optional seed); sovereignty intact |
| **Co-founders** | Clear equity + shipped field wins |
| **Angels/seed** | Visible path to multi-farm ARR + AU entry |
| **Grant funders** | Reported outcomes, jobs/skills, primary sector impact |
| **Māori partners** | Documented benefit-sharing; local data control |

## 12. Maintenance

Update after every material pilot, hire, or raise conversation. Keep comps section dated. **HITL** before sharing externally with investors.

**Related agent skill:** [grants-agent/SKILL.md](grants-agent/SKILL.md) Mode B/C can reference this matrix when drafting equity vs grant paths.
